questions.q: In which city were the Round Table Conferences held?
questions.explanation: The Round Table Conferences were held in London.

questions.q: How many Round Table Conferences were held in total?
questions.explanation: Three Round Table Conferences were held between 1930 and 1932.

questions.q: The Round Table Conferences were called mainly to discuss what for India?
questions.explanation: The conferences were convened to discuss constitutional reform for India.

questions.q: Which Indian leader attended the Second Round Table Conference as the sole Congress representative?
questions.explanation: Gandhi attended the Second Round Table Conference as the sole representative of the Congress.

questions.q: Over what span of years were the Round Table Conferences held?
questions.explanation: The three Round Table Conferences were held between 1930 and 1932.

questions.q: The Round Table Conferences are sometimes simply described as 'Talks in London' about India's:
questions.explanation: The conferences were talks in London about India's future constitution and governance.

questions.q: Which conference did Gandhi attend in person?
questions.explanation: Gandhi attended only the Second Round Table Conference in 1931.

questions.q: The Round Table Conferences were convened by the government of which country?
questions.explanation: The Round Table Conferences were convened by the British government.

questions.q: The First Round Table Conference was notably boycotted by which Indian party?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress boycotted the First Round Table Conference.

questions.q: The Round Table Conferences took place during the broader period of which Indian movement?
questions.explanation: The conferences coincided with the Civil Disobedience Movement in India.

questions.q: In which year was the First Round Table Conference held?
questions.explanation: The First Round Table Conference was held in 1930 (November 1930 to January 1931).

questions.q: Which agreement paved the way for Gandhi to attend the Second Round Table Conference?
questions.explanation: The Gandhi-Irwin Pact of March 1931 enabled Gandhi's participation in the Second Round Table Conference.

questions.q: Which British Prime Minister presided over the Round Table Conferences?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Ramsay MacDonald presided over the Round Table Conferences in London.

questions.q: A major sticking point at the Second Round Table Conference was the question of:
questions.explanation: The communal question of separate electorates and minority representation was a key obstacle at the Second Conference.

questions.q: In which year was the Second Round Table Conference held?
questions.explanation: The Second Round Table Conference was held in 1931.

questions.q: In which year was the Third Round Table Conference held?
questions.explanation: The Third Round Table Conference was held in 1932.

questions.q: Which leader of the depressed classes attended the Round Table Conferences and pressed for their representation?
questions.explanation: B. R. Ambedkar attended the conferences and argued for the political representation of the depressed classes.

questions.q: After the failure of the Second Round Table Conference, Gandhi returned to India and the Congress did what?
questions.explanation: Following the inconclusive Second Conference, the Congress revived the Civil Disobedience Movement in 1932.

questions.q: The Congress boycotted which of the three Round Table Conferences?
questions.explanation: The Congress did not participate in the First or Third Conferences, attending only the Second through Gandhi.

questions.q: The discussions at the Round Table Conferences eventually fed into which major piece of British legislation?
questions.explanation: The conferences contributed to the framing of the Government of India Act 1935.

questions.q: Why was the First Round Table Conference considered limited in its impact?
options: The Congress, the main nationalist body, did not attend
It lasted only a single day
questions.explanation: The First Conference had limited impact because the Congress, the principal nationalist body, boycotted it.

questions.q: At the conferences, princely states were represented by their:
questions.explanation: The princely states were represented at the conferences by their rulers and nominated representatives.

questions.q: The deadlock over minority representation at the Second Conference led the British PM to announce which 1932 award?
questions.explanation: The unresolved communal question led Ramsay MacDonald to announce the Communal Award in 1932.

questions.q: Which woman leader represented Indian women's interests at the Round Table Conferences alongside others?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu was among the women representatives at the Round Table Conferences, attending with Gandhi in 1931.

questions.q: Gandhi's dispute with Ambedkar over the Communal Award's separate electorates for the depressed classes was resolved by which 1932 agreement?
questions.explanation: The 1932 Poona Pact replaced separate electorates for the depressed classes with reserved seats, ending Gandhi's fast.

questions.q: During which months was the Second Round Table Conference primarily held in 1931?
options: October to November of the previous year
questions.explanation: The Second Round Table Conference was held roughly from September to December 1931.

questions.q: The First Round Table Conference notably saw a proposal for an all-India federation involving both British India and the:
questions.explanation: The First Conference floated the idea of an all-India federation uniting British India and the princely states.

questions.q: Which veteran Muslim League leader took part in the Round Table Conferences and championed minority safeguards?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ali Jinnah participated in the Round Table Conferences and argued for safeguards for the Muslim minority.

questions.q: The Third Round Table Conference was the smallest, partly because it was boycotted by the Congress and skipped by some of which group?
questions.explanation: The Third Conference was small and overlooked, with the Congress absent and British Labour representatives staying away.

questions.q: After his return from the Second Conference, Gandhi was arrested in early 1932 under which Viceroy's government?
questions.explanation: Gandhi was arrested in January 1932 under Viceroy Lord Willingdon after returning from the Second Round Table Conference.
